question_text: Why is it easier to open a tin of paint using a long-handled lever than a short-handled one?

options.label: A
options.text: Because the long handle is lighter

options.label: B
options.text: Because a larger distance from the pivot produces a larger moment with the same force

options.label: D
options.text: Because it requires less distance to move

correct_answer: Because a larger distance from the pivot produces a larger moment with the same force
explanation: A moment is a turning effect produced by a force. The larger the distance from the pivot (the turning point), the larger the moment you can create with the same force. This is why a long-handled lever makes it easier to open a tin of paint—you can apply less force but still produce enough turning moment to lift the lid.
